prompt_text: Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience. Access to clean water is a basic human right. Therefore, every home should have a water supply that is provided free of charge. Do you agree or disagree? Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

Sample 0.essay:
In modern society, life has changed  a great deal, especially in water. This includes the free clean water. Some people agree with accessing free clean water, while others disagree with the same points. Both views have significant marks. This essay provides key information to support the topic and present my own opinion.

On the one hand, in terms of the agreements, according to the given data, there are many significant points. This is due to  positive reasons. First, the free clean water prevents the number of poor people. This is due to the free charge of water. Second, the free water can increase the green area. This is due to the higher number of free water tools. For example, the park becomes green and fresh. Moreover, the temperature level becomes colder.

On the other hand, in terms of the disagreements, according to the given information, there are many dramatic points. This is due to the negative reasons. Firstly, the percentage of free charge drink in the homes is highly expensive. This will damage the economy of the city. As a result, the rate of the cost becomes lower. For example, the level of the school drink will be low.

In conclusion, according to the agreements and disagreements, according to the recent reports, there are many benefits. However, there are many drawbacks. Both marks have significant data. Thus, the government recommends people follow the tips for a positive result, a great life and a right stability. In my opinion, the free aquatic product provides a perfect impact.

A.name: Task Response
A.feedback: 5.5 — The candidate addresses the task (discusses both views and gives an opinion) but development is weak and unclear. Ideas are repetitive and often irrelevant (e.g. “the free clean water prevents the number of poor people” lacks clear reasoning). Examples are vague and not well explained, so the response only partially fulfils task demands.

B.name: Coherence & Cohesion
B.feedback: 5.5 — There is a visible overall paragraph structure (introduction, body, conclusion) and use of linking phrases (‘On the one hand/On the other hand’). However cohesion is mechanical and over‑reliant on set phrases; progression is unclear and some sentences/ideas are poorly connected (awkward reference and sequencing: “This is due to the given data,” repeated).

C.name: Lexical Resource
C.feedback: 5.0 — Vocabulary range is limited and there are frequent inappropriate word choices and collocation errors (e.g. “free charge of water,” “free water tools,” “free aquatic product,” “percentage of free charge drink”). Repetition of simple words and many inaccurate phrases reduce precision and clarity.

D.name: Grammar Range & Accuracy
D.feedback: 6.0 — The essay shows an ability to produce a mix of simple and some complex sentence forms. Many sentences are grammatically adequate, so meaning is usually clear, but there are frequent errors in article use, prepositions and word order (e.g. “life has changed a great deal, especially in water,” “the level of the school drink will be low”). Errors occur regularly but do not completely obscure meaning.

prompt_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements ? with the help of technology , students nowadays can learn more information and learn it more quickly.

Sample 0.essay:
Nowadays, adavacement of technology helps students to gain alot of information in short period of time. I strongly believe with the statement because of various educational softwares and online educational data. 

There are different types of educational applications  which helps to learn fast and more. These softwares have different elements, such as practice the questions, recorded lectures and recorded procedures. Therefore, with the help of these apps students can practice weak areas and get evaluations for their performance. For example, there is a app called archerreview, nursing students widely use it for their board exam preparations. This app has very unique features which allow students to give realistic experience of actual exam. Moreover, it has recorded lectures, both detailed and short videos, which learner can use according to need.

On the internet there are many websites which gives wide and sophisticated information on specific topics. These sites are offical carry original and legit information. Students have opportunities to download documents and save it for later use. For instance, Ilets websites such as cambrige, bristish council and IDP post their test exams. They also allows them to pratice tests. They keeping posting the updates on any type of changes in the pattern or ways of carry exams etc.

In the conclusion, It is belived that technolgy plays a vital role in helping students to learn fast and more these days. I highly agrees because some apps and site are very useful engines to learn.

A.name: Task Response
A.feedback: Band 3.0 — Candidate addresses the task and expresses a clear position (agrees) with some supporting points and examples (ArcherReview, IELTS sites). However ideas are under‑developed, repetitive and sometimes unclear (e.g. vague claims like 'learn fast and more' without explanation), so the response only partially develops arguments.

B.name: Coherence & Cohesion
B.feedback: Band 3.0 — The essay has a basic paragraph structure and some linking words (Therefore, Moreover, For instance) but overall logical progression is weak. Sentences and paragraphs are repetitive and connections are often unclear (poor transitions and sequencing: e.g. mixing features and uses without clear organisation).

C.name: Lexical Resource
C.feedback: Band 2.5 — Frequent lexical errors, misspellings and wrong word forms reduce clarity (adavacement, alot, softwares, offical, pratice, technolgy, Ilets, cambrige, bristish). Vocabulary range is limited and collocations are often incorrect ('give realistic experience', 'useful engines to learn'), which impedes communication.

D.name: Grammar Range & Accuracy
D.feedback: Band 3.5 — There is some variety in sentence types and attempt at complex sentences, but frequent grammar errors (article mistakes: 'a app', plural/singular: 'which helps', agreement: 'websites which gives'), awkward word order and punctuation mistakes occur. Despite errors, overall meaning is usually recoverable.

prompt_text:
For schoolchildren, their teachers have more 
 influence on their intelligence and social devel
 opment than their parents. To what extent do 
 you agree or disagree?

Sample 0.essay:
Both teachers and parents play a significant role in a child’s upbringing. In this essay, I will discuss why I disagree
 that teachers are a greater influence on the intelligence and social development of children than their parents.
 The first few years of a child’s life are spent almost entirely in the company of their parental figures. These
 years are critical in the social development of a child, as they contain their first attempts to communicate with
 other people in order to receive the care and attention necessary for survival. Most school children start their
 education between the ages of four to seven years. Therefore, they have already experienced a considerable
 amount of parental influence before teachers are even introduced to them.
 Other significant factors in the intelligence and social development of a child are the socioeconomic circumstances
 that they are brought up in. The quality of a child’s social life, schooling and health are all socioeconomic factors that
 heavily influence a child. These factors are a direct consequence of the circumstances brought about by the child’s
 parents. Better teachers will endow a child with greater intelligence and social skills, however, better teachers are, on
 average, found at better schools. The attendance of a child at a good school is usually the consequence of a decision
 made by their parents.
 I believe that parents are a far greater influence on the intelligence and social development of children than
 teachers because, firstly, the early developmental years of a child’s life are almost entirely influenced by the
 company of their parents and, secondly, because the socioeconomic factors of a child’s life are usually a direct
 consequence of the factors which effect their parents.

A.name: Task Response
A.feedback: Bài trả lời đầy đủ yêu cầu, rõ ràng nêu quan điểm (disagree) và phát triển 2 luận điểm hỗ trợ (giai đoạn đầu đời, yếu tố kinh tế-xã hội). Luận điểm được trình bày mạch lạc, không có thiếu sót về mặt nội dung; có thể bổ sung ví dụ cụ thể nhưng không cần thiết để đáp ứng đề.

B.name: Coherence & Cohesion
B.feedback: Cấu trúc đoạn rõ ràng (mở bài, thân bài 2 đoạn, kết luận), liên kết ý tốt (therefore, firstly, secondly) và trình tự logic. Có lặp ý nhẹ ở kết luận nhưng không ảnh hưởng tới tính mạch lạc tổng thể.

C.name: Lexical Resource
C.feedback: Từ vựng phong phú và phù hợp cho chủ đề (upbringing, socioeconomic circumstances, parental figures, endow). Có một lỗi từ vựng nhỏ/đảo từ (ví dụ “which effect their parents” — nên là “affect”); tuy nhiên phạm vi từ vựng rộng và chính xác ở hầu hết các chỗ.

D.name: Grammar Range & Accuracy
D.feedback: Sử dụng đa dạng cấu trúc câu phức tạp và kiểm soát ngữ pháp rất tốt. Lỗi ngữ pháp nhỏ, không hệ thống (ví dụ “which effect their parents” – sai dùng “effect” thay vì “affect”; “between the ages of four to seven” có thể dùng “and”), nhưng không làm giảm hiểu nghĩa.
